content.text: I found the two relevant paths: same-type deep cloning can fall into `CtorMappingBuilder` (single-argument copy constructor), while normal object-member construction later honors `[MapperConstructor]`. I’m checking the builder order and test harness details before adding a focused regression test.

I found that same-type deep cloning reaches `CtorMappingBuilder` before object-member mapping, so single-argument copy constructors are selected before `[MapperConstructor]` participates in object constructor resolution. I’ll add focused regression tests now to confirm both explicit and record-synthesized copy constructor cases.

Implemented the fix in:

- `src/Riok.Mapperly/Descriptors/MappingBuilders/CtorMappingBuilder.cs`

Change made:
- Constructor mapping (`new Target(source)`, including record copy constructors exposed through unsafe accessors) is now skipped when the target type has any constructor marked with `[MapperConstructor]`.
- This lets the normal object-member mapping path run, where `[MapperConstructor]` is prioritized and `[MapProperty(Use = ...)]` mappings are applied.

Validation performed:
- Added temporary regression tests for:
  - Class with explicit copy constructor + `[MapperConstructor]`
  - Record with synthesized copy constructor + `[MapperConstructor]`
- Confirmed they failed before the fix and passed after the fix.

